future_bounded_patch_surface.summary: A future Track A patch may target only the bounded pointer-residual surfaces already evidenced by the split packet and must remain isolated from IF08 route text review, expectation rebasing, and legacy pruning.
deferred_surfaces_not_in_first_track_a_p: PRE-F21 artifacts/v5 lineage pruning or replacement

future_patch_entry_criteria: A separate future operator authorization must explicitly open a bounded Track A Project_ARIS patch packet before any code or test file mutation.

Immediately before any future patch, git -C /home/matheus/ARIS_RECOVERY_20260612/Project_ARIS-clean-reclone rev-parse HEAD must still equal 7c0dc8d14fcbc2bc4246282c7ebf8b0896622dad or a replacement cleanroom packet must admit a new clean head explicitly.
Immediately before any future patch, git -C /home/matheus/ARIS_RECOVERY_20260612/Project_ARIS-clean-reclone status --short must still be empty.
Immediately before any future patch, find /home/matheus/ARIS_RECOVERY_20260612/Project_ARIS-clean-reclone -maxdepth 1 -name aris-active-context must return nothing.
The dirty quarantine clone at /home/matheus/ARIS_RECOVERY_20260612/Project_ARIS-clean must remain untouched and must not be reused.
The future patch must remain bounded to the seven Track A candidate files unless a separate scope-expansion packet is authorized first.

The future patch must preserve ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json, ACTIVE_CONTEXT_POINTER.md, .gitmodules, .env*, archive/**, excludent/**, secrets/**, dependency files, and any IF08 route-text surface unchanged.
future_patch_rejection_criteria: Any future step attempts to patch Project_ARIS without a separate explicit authorization beyond this readiness packet.
Any future step re-creates a repository-root aris-active-context/ directory in the fresh reclone.
Any future step widens into IF08 route or canonical-text changes.
Any future step widens into expectation-drift rebasing for tests/test_phase_completion_materialization_guard.py or scripts/check_phase_completion_materialization.py.
Any future step widens into PRE-F21 legacy pruning without a separate authorization packet.
Any future step treats a dirty fresh reclone, a changed head, or nested active-context residue as acceptable admission state.
Any future step claims remediation_proven=true, finding close, real_apply, runtime execution, product work, Bedrock work, or secrets access from this Track A lineage.
future_forbidden_now: Any command that edits /home/matheus/ARIS/Project_ARIS/** from this readiness step
Any command that edits /home/matheus/ARIS_RECOVERY_20260612/Project_ARIS-clean/** or /home/matheus/ARIS_RECOVERY_20260612/Project_ARIS-clean-reclone/** from this readiness step
Any patch against IF08 W0.5 route/canonical-text sources
Any runtime execution, remediation apply retry, real_apply, product, Bedrock, secrets, or finding-close action
